description: A memory is the complete list of skills available to an entity. It defines what an entity CAN do, not what it WILL do. The decision of when/how to use a skill is handled by the entity's controller (brain), not by the memory.
design_principles: Memory = list of skill IDs the entity can access
Memory is portable — it can be loaded by any engine or described to a human GM
The player starts with only ver_guia and gains skills through the Guide
NPCs have fixed memories that define their behavior repertoire
Memory can be exported mid-session to continue on another platform
Equipment adds skills to memory (a weapon may unlock weapon-specific skills)

memory_schema.entity_id: Reference to the entity this memory belongs to
memory_schema.innate_skills: Skills the entity is born with. Cannot be removed.
memory_schema.learned_skills: Skills acquired through gameplay. Can be gained and lost.
memory_schema.equipment_skills: Skills temporarily available from equipped items. Removed on unequip.
memory_schema.active_slots: Which skills are mapped to which input slots (player only)
memory_schema.skill_allocations: Points invested in each skill (improves that skill)
memory_schema.state: Current HP, stamina, position, active statuses, cooldowns — the live session state

portability.export_format: A memory + state snapshot can be serialized to JSON and loaded elsewhere
portability.tabletop_conversion: In tabletop mode, the GM reads the memory as the character sheet. Cooldowns become round counts. Stamina becomes action economy.
portability.cross_engine: The same memory can be loaded by Godot, Unity, a world model, or a human GM. The engine maps skill atoms to its own execution system.
